title: Mistakes Were Made (But Not by Me)
subtitle: Why We Justify Foolish Beliefs, Bad Decisions, and Hurtful Acts
author: Carol Tavris & Elliot Aronson
themes: Cognitive Dissonance as the Engine of Self-Justification
Blind Spot Bias — Seeing Bias in Others, Not Ourselves
Dissonance in Relationships, Law, Medicine, and Politics
excerpt: Why is it so hard to say 'I made a mistake' — and really believe it? Social psychologists Carol Tavris and Elliot Aronson reveal the engine behind our most stubborn blind spots: cognitive dissonance. When our actions clash with our self-image, we don't admit error — we rewrite history, blame others, and double down. From failed prophecies to wrongful convictions to broken marriages, this book traces how self-justification shapes every corner of human life — and what it takes to break free.
